channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Tennessee teens sue Elon Musk's xAI over AI-generated child sexual abuse material
item.description: The three girls say that the nonconsensual nude images were created by a perpetrator who used AI company xAI's image generation tools.
item.content:encoded:
The three girls say that the nonconsensual nude images were created by a perpetrator who used AI company xAI's image generation tools.

(Image credit: Nicolas Tucat)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: Afghanistan says 400 people killed in Pakistan strike on Kabul hospital
item.description: Afghanistan has accused Pakistan of targeting a hospital for drug users in the Afghan capital with an airstrike, marking a dramatic escalation of a conflict that began late last month. Pakistan has dismissed the accusation.
item.content:encoded:
Afghanistan has accused Pakistan of targeting a hospital for drug users in the Afghan capital with an airstrike, marking a dramatic escalation of a conflict that began late last month. Pakistan has dismissed the accusation.

(Image credit: Barackatullah Popal)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Federal judge halts RFK Jr.'s changes to children's vaccine policies
item.description: In a rebuke, a federal district court judge blocked the administration's reduction in the number of immunizations recommended for kids and also changes to an influential vaccine committee.
item.content:encoded:
In a rebuke, a federal district court judge blocked the administration's reduction in the number of immunizations recommended for kids and also changes to an influential vaccine committee.

(Image credit: Samuel Corum)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Supreme Court to hear expedited arguments on protected status for migrants
item.description: The court temporarily blocked the Trump administration from deporting some 6,000 Syrians and 350,000 Haitians who were granted Temporary Protected Status.
item.content:encoded:
The court temporarily blocked the Trump administration from deporting some 6,000 Syrians and 350,000 Haitians who were granted Temporary Protected Status.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Zoe Sobel

item.title: A new drug could be the beginning of the end for sleeping sickness
item.description: The goal in the world of global health is to bring an end to this scourge by 2030. A new drug looks as if it could do the job.
item.content:encoded:
The goal in the world of global health is to bring an end to this scourge by 2030. A new drug looks as if it could do the job.

(Image credit: Patrick Robert/Corbis/Sygma)
item.dc:creator: Fran Kritz

item.title: Cuba hit by island-wide blackout as energy crisis deepens
item.description: On Monday Cuba was plunged into an island-wide blackout affecting 11 million people after a "complete disconnection" of its electrical system, officials said, amid a worsening fuel shortage.
item.content:encoded:
On Monday Cuba was plunged into an island-wide blackout affecting 11 million people after a "complete disconnection" of its electrical system, officials said, amid a worsening fuel shortage.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Vaccine critics keep the pressure on, even as RFK Jr. shifts focus
item.description: Anti-vaccine activists rally supporters to try to keep the momentum going on changing federal vaccine policies. This comes even as the White House tries to tamp down attention to the unpopular issue ahead of the midterm elections, and a powerful federal advisory committee plans to meet to consider even more moves.
item.content:encoded:
Anti-vaccine activists rally supporters to try to keep the momentum going on changing federal vaccine policies. This comes even as the White House tries to tamp down attention to the unpopular issue ahead of the midterm elections, and a powerful federal advisory committee plans to meet to consider even more moves.

(Image credit: Creative Images Lab)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Gasoline prices are still rising as the Iran war stretches into its third week
item.description: U.S. gasoline prices are up nearly 80 cents from a month ago, while diesel prices have shot up even more. Diesel is now just under $5 a gallon, according to AAA, up $1.34 from last month.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. gasoline prices are up nearly 80 cents from a month ago, while diesel prices have shot up even more. Diesel is now just under $5 a gallon, according to AAA, up $1.34 from last month.

(Image credit: Lindsey Wasson)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Team USA won the second-most medals at these Paralympics. See the standout moments
item.description: A mix of decorated veterans and rising stars won 24 medals for Team USA, 13 of them gold. The last one arrived Sunday, when the U.S. sled hockey team beat Canada to win its fifth straight gold medal.
item.content:encoded:
A mix of decorated veterans and rising stars won 24 medals for Team USA, 13 of them gold. The last one arrived Sunday, when the U.S. sled hockey team beat Canada to win its fifth straight gold medal.

(Image credit: Stefano Rellandini)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: There's room for everyone in 'Now I Surrender,' an epic American Western
item.description: Mexican novelist Álvaro Enrigue re-imagines the story of the American West — and the Apache fight for survival — in an epic that's both defiantly challenging and, at times, magical.
item.content:encoded: Mexican novelist Álvaro Enrigue re-imagines the story of the American West — and the Apache fight for survival — in an epic that's both defiantly challenging and, at times, magical.
item.dc:creator: Maureen Corrigan
